# UI/UX Designer with Figma Skill

## Overview
Professional UI/UX design expertise with Figma proficiency, Material Design 3 mastery, and comprehensive knowledge of design systems, user research, prototyping, and cross-platform design implementation.

## Trigger Conditions
This skill should be used when:
- User asks about UI/UX design, Figma, or design systems
- Tasks involve creating mockups, wireframes, or prototypes
- Design system creation, component libraries, or design tokens
- Questions about Material Design 3, accessibility, or responsive design
- User testing, design research, or usability analysis
- Mobile app design (Android, iOS) or web interface design
- Design documentation, handoff to developers, or design critique
- Animation, interaction design, or micro-interactions

## Core Competencies

### Figma Expertise
- Workspace organization and team collaboration
- Vector drawing and shape tools mastery
- Text styling and typography management
- Color systems and design tokens
- Auto layout for responsive, adaptive designs
- Constraints and resizing behaviors
- Interactive prototyping and component states
- Plugins and automation tools
- Dev mode for developer handoff
- Version history and branching strategies

### Design Systems
- Atomic design methodology (atoms, molecules, organisms)
- Design tokens (colors, typography, spacing, shadows)
- Component libraries and documentation
- Pattern libraries and reusable components
- Design system governance and scalability
- Consistency and brand identity maintenance
- Component variation and state management
- Documentation best practices

### UI Design Principles
- Color theory and accessibility (WCAG compliance)
- Typography and font pairing strategies
- Whitespace and layout principles
- Visual hierarchy and emphasis
- Icon design and systems
- Dark mode and theme design
- Micro-interactions and subtle animations
- Visual consistency across platforms

### User Experience Design
- User research and discovery methods
- User interviews, surveys, and testing
- Competitive analysis and benchmarking
- Wireframing and user flow mapping
- Information architecture (IA) design
- User personas and journey mapping
- Prototyping and interaction design
- Usability testing methodologies
- A/B testing and analytics interpretation
- Heatmaps and user behavior analysis

### Mobile App Design (Android/iOS)
- Material Design 3 implementation
- Android screen densities and guidelines (mdpi, hdpi, xhdpi, etc.)
- iOS Human Interface Guidelines (HIG)
- Navigation patterns (bottom nav, drawer, tabs, rails)
- Safe area and notch handling
- Status bar and system integration
- Gesture design and affordances
- Touch target sizing (minimum 48x48dp for Android)
- Theme and dark mode design

### Responsive Design
- Breakpoint planning (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Mobile-first design approach
- Adaptive layouts in Figma
- Flexible components and scaling
- Fluid typography and spacing
- Cross-device testing and consistency

### Prototyping & Interaction Design
- High-fidelity prototyping in Figma
- Interactive component states (normal, hover, active, disabled, error)
- Animation timing, easing, and curves
- Transition design and navigation flows
- State management in prototypes
- User flow visualization and logic
- Clickable prototypes and user testing

### Design Communication
- Design specifications and annotations
- Component documentation
- Design token export and management
- Developer handoff best practices
- Figma Dev mode usage
- Design system versioning
- Design critique methodology
- Stakeholder communication

### Design Thinking
- Problem-solving frameworks
- Problem statement definition
- Ideation and brainstorming sessions
- Solution validation and testing
- Design iteration and refinement
- Design trends and innovations
- Voice UI and emerging technologies
- Inclusive design practices

## Example Usage Scenarios
1. **Creating a design system**: "Help me design a complete Material Design 3 component library in Figma for our Android app"
2. **Mobile app design**: "Design the user interface for an Android e-commerce app with bottom navigation and Material guidelines"
3. **Prototype testing**: "Create an interactive prototype for user testing with multiple screens and navigation flows"
4. **Dark mode**: "Design a cohesive dark mode theme for our existing light-mode design system"
5. **Accessibility**: "Ensure our design meets WCAG accessibility standards with proper color contrast and font sizes"
6. **Design tokens**: "Set up a design token system in Figma with colors, typography, and spacing"
7. **Figma components**: "Build reusable Figma components with variants for different states and sizes"
8. **Handoff**: "Prepare design specifications for developer handoff with annotations and component documentation"

## Best Practices
- Follow Material Design 3 guidelines for Android apps
- Prioritize accessibility in all designs (WCAG AA minimum)
- Use design tokens for maintainability and consistency
- Create reusable components with clear documentation
- Test designs with real users before implementation
- Iterate based on feedback and analytics
- Maintain design system up-to-date with implementations
- Document design decisions and rationale
- Use Figma Dev mode for seamless developer collaboration
- Consider performance impacts of animations and effects
- Design for offline states, error states, and loading states
- Test on actual devices and screen sizes

## Related Skills
- Android Kotlin Developer (for implementation of designs)
- iOS Developer (for cross-platform design consistency)
- Frontend Designer (for web interface design)
- User Research (for deeper user insights)
- Accessibility specialist (for compliance standards)
